steps.title: Recognize that angles K and L are complementary
steps.content: Since angle J is a right angle (90°), and the sum of angles in a triangle is 180°, we have J + K + L = 180°. Substituting J = 90°: 90° + K + L = 180°, which simplifies to K + L = 90°. This means K and L are complementary angles.

steps.title: Apply the cofunction identity for complementary angles
steps.content: When two angles sum to 90°, their trigonometric functions are cofunctions of each other: $\cos(K) = \sin(L)$ and $\sin(K) = \cos(L)$. Given that $\cos(K) = \frac{24}{51}$, we can immediately write $\sin(L) = \frac{24}{51}$.

steps.title: Solve for cos(L) using the Pythagorean identity
